description: Core utility APIs available inside p5sheet scripts: canvas() for initialization, fields proxy for studio variable binding, phaseStepper() for interactive step navigation, bridge() for executing studio expressions from JavaScript, and effect() for animations. NOT standalone expressions.
utility: Core p5sheet script utilities: canvas, fields, phaseStepper, bridge

alternates.description: Must be called in setup() to create the p5 canvas. The canvas size is determined by the p5sheet's view(height, width) expression. Without calling canvas(), nothing will render.

A Proxy object that reads and writes studio expression variables. Reading: fields.a returns the current value of variable a (undefined if not set). Writing: fields.a = 5 sets variable a to 5. Changes are reactive and update other expressions that depend on the variable. Use with inputstepper or inputnumber expressions to create interactive controls.

Creates an interactive step navigation UI with clickable circles at the bottom of the canvas. Properties: .phase (current phase number, 0 means no phase active, 1 to stepCount). .phaseFrames (frames per transition, default 30). Methods: .phaseT(p) returns animation progress 0-1 for phase p (0 if not reached, smooth interpolation during transition, 1 if past), .handleClick() call in mousePressed() to handle circle clicks, .draw() call at end of draw() to render step circles.

Returns a TypedCommandBridge for executing studio expressions from JavaScript. Methods: .execute(varName, exprStr) executes any expression string, .noAnimate() disables animation, .animate() re-enables animation. Typed shortcuts: .g2d(name, { row, col, size, rowHeight, xRange, type }), .point(name, { graph, x, y }), .line(name, { graph, x1, y1, x2, y2 }), .segment(name, opts), .ray(name, opts), .vector(name, opts), .circle(name, { graph, cx, cy, r }), .angle(name, { graph, vertex, p1, p2 }), .plot(name, { graph, equation }), .paraplot(name, { graph, xEquation, yEquation, tMin, tMax }), .polarplot(name, { graph, equation }), .impplot(name, { graph, equation }), .inequalityplot(name, { graph, equation }), .piecewiseplot(name, { graph, pieces }), .notes(name, { text, effect, vpos, color, fillColor, fontSize, fontFamily, padding, stayDuration, ref }), .media(name, { asset, startTime, duration, volume, speed, ref }), .comment(name, { targets, target, text, placement, color, fillColor, fontSize, fontFamily, duration, buffX, buffY }).
